samples.raw_output:
<think>The user wants me to classify the headline "Apple unveils new M5 chip with on-device transformer accelerator" into one of the given categories: tech, sports, politics, health, entertainment.

This headline is clearly about:
- Apple (a technology company)
- A new M5 chip (computer hardware/processor)
- On-device transformer accelerator (AI/ML hardware feature)

This is definitely a technology-related news story. The answer should be "tech".
</think>

tech

<think>The user wants me to classify the headline "Real Madrid sign teenage striker from Brazilian league for record fee" into one of the given categories: tech, sports, politics, health, entertainment.

This headline is about:
- Real Madrid (a football/soccer club)
- Signing a player (transfer)
- A striker (football position)
- From the Brazilian league
- A record fee (transfer fee)

This is clearly about sports - specifically football/soccer transfer news.

I should respond with just one word: sports
</think>

sports

<think>The user wants me to classify the headline "Senate passes bipartisan bill on critical infrastructure cybersecurity" into one of the given categories: tech, sports, politics, health, entertainment.

Let me analyze this headline:
- "Senate passes" - This refers to a legislative body, which is political
- "bipartisan bill" - This is clearly about politics/government
- "critical infrastructure cybersecurity" - While this involves technology/cybersecurity, the main focus of the headline is about the Senate passing a bill, which is a political action

The primary topic here is the political action of passing a bill, even though the subject matter involves cybersecurity. The headline is about government/legislative action, which falls under politics.

I should classify this as "politics".
</think>

politics

<think>The user wants me to classify the headline "WHO warns of measles resurgence in countries with declining vaccination rates" into one of the given categories: tech, sports, politics, health, entertainment.

Let me analyze this headline:
- WHO (World Health Organization) is a health organization
- It talks about measles resurgence
- It mentions vaccination rates
- This is clearly about health/medical issues

This should be classified as "health" as it deals with disease, vaccination, and public health warnings.
</think>

health

- Opening weekend performance (movie release)

This is clearly about entertainment - specifically movies/film industry news.

- "GPU-accelerated query planner" - This is a technical feature related to computing/databases

This is clearly a technology-related headline. It's about a software/database technology update.
